title: The basics
assignment: Review the concepts from this section. If something is unclear, go back to the lesson.
groups.0.title: Tags and elements
groups.0.items.0.def:
opens.

closes. The slash says "this is where it ends". Forget it, and the browser guesses where the thing ends — and it usually guesses wrong.
groups.0.items.1.def: Opening tag + content + closing tag. The three together.
groups.0.items.2.def:
The text sits INSIDE the tag, not after it.

Ivan

, not

Ivan.
groups.1.title: The tags you know
groups.1.items.0.def: Top-level heading. It does not mean "big" — it means "important". The size is the browser's call.
groups.1.items.1.def: Paragraph. Without it, sentences glue into one line, no matter how many Enters you press.
groups.2.title: What you are actually doing
groups.2.items.0.term: You describe, you do not draw
groups.2.items.0.def: You never say how big, what font, what colour. You only say what a thing IS. How it looks is the browser's call.
groups.2.items.1.def: The Enters you see in your code are not there for the browser. If you want separation, you say so with a tag.
